# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account and generates a JSON key file for application authentication. Include the key creation in the template so applications can authenticate immediately. This is for our application services that need GCP API access from outside GCP.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

apiVersion: cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: DeploymentManager
metadata:
  name: my-deploymentmanager
spec:
  template: | # start of the YAML content, not a real comment at all! (just so it gets marked as code)
    container: >
      image: gcr.io/cloud-marketplace/servicemanager_withkeygen@sha256:78c1ec4f3b09aadabdffcfbaefcefddeeaeacbfcaacafebeebeabcbbbcdefeeeedea
      command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"] # Starts the container's process. This runs in an init-containers and is run first, before main (unless otherwise specified).  The "/main" part of "command": ["my_service","--someOption=true"]. Also used to handle any command line arguments passed during initialization
      securityContext: # defines how the container should be sandboxed. Only root user inside a process can gain privileges over this mechanism and is therefore not required when we just want services running as one or less privileged users (like in local development).  All commands have setuid bit which requires sudo permissions to run
        capabilities: {} # Defines the access control for container's processes. This section only works on Linux containers, because these are based around SecurityContext defined above and its not used with Windows Containers/Podman yet (only local user namespace is supported). The default value here would be an empty object which leaves all capabilities unrestricted
      readiness: # defines the period when container should report back to Kubernetes that it's ready. If set, in millisecond format(10ms) or not at all allowed but used with caution as a signal for start-up scripts and services (for example from an `init` process).
        delayBeforeStart: 5m # how long should we wait before the Kubernetes container is ready to make it available? For non Linux containers. The default value here would be '0s' or not at all allowed but used with caution as a signal for start-up scripts and services (for example from an `init` process).
  parameters: # No inputs yet, so no explicit content below! Add the required values by changing references to $() in comments. These should contain actual information such as names or email addresses etc that would be included when deploying this template via DeploymentManager API if we needed them passed explicitly through Kubernetes's parameterization of templates and instances
    - name: projectID # Reference the Project ID provided by GCP from where you want to create these services. Replace with your actual value ($PROJECT_NAME). Ejm "project-123456"  or $(yourCloudFunctionsProject) etc depending on how it was passed in
    - name: region # This refers to the specific geographic location where you want this service and its resources. Replace with your actual value ($REGION). Ejm "us-central1", "europe-west2" or $(yourCloudComputeRegion) etc depending on how it was passed in
    - name: keyfileName # Name of the JSON file where will be stored generated Key from service account. Replace with your actual value ($KEYFILE_NAME). Ejm 'mykey'  as an example if you want to use this filename for authentication purpose
